title: Scroll Scopes, Docks, Highlights, Goo & Anchors
description: Five fresh browser-native patterns: timeline-scope cross-DOM scroll sync, a :has()-powered dock, the Highlight API, a goo cursor trail, and anchor-positioned popovers.

drops.title: Cross-Branch Scroll Sync
drops.description: Scrolling a list drives an animated ring, bar, and badge on a completely unrelated branch of the DOM — zero JS.
drops.concept: timeline-scope 'hoists' a named scroll-timeline out of the subtree that defines it, so any element inside a shared ancestor can subscribe via animation-timeline — even if it isn't a descendant of the scroller. This is the 2026 evolution of scroll-driven animation beyond simple parent-to-child effects.
drops.source: master.dev (Frontend Masters Blog) — "Named Scroll & View Timelines"

interaction.mouse: Scroll the left feed to drive the right-side panels
interaction.click: Toggle timeline-scope on/off in the debug panel
interaction.touch: Scroll the feed with a finger

drops.title: CSS-Only Dock Magnifier
drops.description: A macOS-style dock that magnifies whatever you hover and cascades a falloff to both neighbors, in pure CSS.
drops.concept: Traditional sibling combinators (+, ~) only look forward in the DOM. Wrapping one inside :has() on a preceding element lets it react to a sibling hovered after it, enabling a backward falloff — no JS required for a dock that used to need a whole library.

interaction.mouse: Hover any dock icon to see the falloff
interaction.touch: Tap an icon to magnify it

drops.title: Highlight-API Word Paint
drops.description: Hover, scrub, or search a paragraph and individual words light up — no span ever touches the DOM.
drops.concept: The CSS Custom Highlight API lets JS mark arbitrary text Ranges and style them purely through ::highlight(), decoupling text-effect logic from DOM structure. It's the mechanism now powering fast in-browser syntax highlighters and search-match UIs.
drops.source: pavi2410.com — "High-Performance Syntax Highlighting with CSS Highlights API"

interaction.mouse: Move over passage 1 to paint the word underneath
interaction.click: Type in the search box to highlight matches

drops.title: Gooey Cursor Trail
drops.description: Trailing blobs melt into one liquid shape as the cursor moves, with a click-burst and an ambient lava-lamp variant.
drops.concept: An SVG filter blurs overlapping shapes then re-sharpens the alpha channel via a contrast matrix, so nearby shapes visually merge instead of alpha-blending. Paired with lerp-based trailing dots, it produces an organic liquid-metal cursor — newly popular again in 2026 portfolio sites.

interaction.mouse: Move inside the box to trail blobs
interaction.click: Click for a gooey particle burst
interaction.touch: Drag a finger inside the box

drops.title: Anchor-Positioned Popover Menu
drops.description: A nested dropdown menu with a submenu, built entirely from the Popover API and CSS anchor positioning.
drops.concept: popovertarget wires open/close and focus trapping natively; anchor-name / position-anchor / position-area replace Floating-UI-style JS math for placement, including nested submenus anchored to their own trigger button.
drops.source: css-irl.info — "Anchor Positioning and the Popover API for a JS-Free Site Menu"

interaction.mouse: Click a menu button to open it
interaction.click: Click "More ▸" to open the nested submenu
